name: Pork with Stuffed Yams
recipeIngredient: 4 pork loin or rib chops, about 3/4-inch thick
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Prick yams with a fork to allow steam to escape.
3. Bake yams for 55 to 60 minutes or until tender.
4. Trim fat from pork chops.
5. About 30 minutes before the yams are done, place pork in an ungreased rectangular pan, 13 X 9 X 2 inches.
6. Mix salt, paprika, garlic powder and pepper; sprinkle half of this mixture over pork.
7. Turn off heat and sprinkle with remaining seasoning mixture.
8. Pour 1/2 cup orange juice into the pan.
9. Cover and bake for 30 minutes.
10. Cut yams lengthwise in half and scoop out pulp, leaving a 1/4-inch shell.
11. Mash the pulp; beat in 2 tablespoons orange juice until light and fluffy.
12. Stir in apple, onion, and celery.
13. Fill shells with pulp mixture and push pork to one end of the pan.
14. Place yams at the other end.
15. Bake uncovered for about 30 minutes or until pork is tender and yams are hot.
4 servings
